title: Uncle Ho Leaves to Find a Way to Save the Nation
summary: The young man Nguyen Tat Thanh, with his bare hands, began a journey of ten thousand miles on a merchant ship to find enlightenment for the nation.

slides.text: In 1911, the young man Nguyen Tat Thanh (Nguyen Sinh Cung's new name) decided to leave his homeland for the West to find a new path to national salvation.
slides.imagePrompt: Nguyen Tat Thanh in a simple brown suit, standing at the Nha Rong wharf, looking at a large steamship, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: At Nha Rong Wharf (Saigon), he applied for a job as a kitchen assistant on a French merchant ship named Amiral Latouche-Tréville under the name Van Ba.
slides.imagePrompt: Văn Ba (Nguyen Tat Thanh) carrying a heavy bag onto a ship, determined face, busy dock scene, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Despite the extremely hard work, from early morning until late at night, he never forgot his single goal: to find out what other countries did to save their own nations.
slides.imagePrompt: Van Ba working in the ship's kitchen, steam rising, looking out of a small window at the sea, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: June 5, 1911, became a historic milestone. Uncle Ho's 30-year journey across the world began here!
slides.imagePrompt: The large ship sailing out into the wide ocean, sunset, a trail of white foam, a symbol of a new era beginning, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: From which wharf did Uncle Ho leave to go abroad?
In what year did Uncle Ho go abroad to find a way to save the nation?
